title: Sarcoglottis neuroptera
intro: Sarcoglottis neuroptera, also known as Butterfly Orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Sarcoglottis neuroptera include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are employed to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Sarcoglottis neuroptera is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This classification places it within the monocot order Asparagales. The Orchidaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families in the plant kingdom. Sarcoglottis neuroptera is further classified into the subfamily Orchidoideae. Its classification as a member of the Orchidaceae family is supported by morphological and molecular evidence.
